abound.en: exist in large numbers or amounts

ex.en: Wildlife abounds in this national park.
ex.ko: 이 국립공원에는 야생동물이 넘쳐난다.

abstraction.en: an idea or quality considered apart from any specific object or instance

ex.en: Freedom is an abstraction that is hard to define precisely.
ex.ko: 자유란 명확히 정의하기 어려운 추상적 개념이다.

abstraction.en: a state of being deeply lost in thought and unaware of one's surroundings

ex.en: He answered in a moment of abstraction, barely aware of what she had said.
ex.ko: 그는 딴생각에 빠진 채 그녀가 무슨 말을 했는지도 모르고 대답했다.

accustomed.en: used to something because you have experienced it often

ex.en: She quickly became accustomed to the noise of the city.
ex.ko: 그녀는 도시의 소음에 금방 익숙해졌다.

ex.en: He took his accustomed seat by the window.
ex.ko: 그는 늘 앉던 창가 자리에 앉았다.

acidic.en: having the properties of an acid; having a pH below 7

ex.en: The soil here is quite acidic.
ex.ko: 이곳 토양은 상당히 산성이다.

ex.en: The wine had an acidic aftertaste.
ex.ko: 그 와인은 뒷맛이 신맛이 났다.

acoustic.en: relating to sound or the sense of hearing

ex.en: The hall has excellent acoustic properties.
ex.ko: 그 홀은 음향 특성이 뛰어나다.

acoustic.cue: 전자 증폭 장치를 쓰지 않는 악기

ex.en: He prefers playing an acoustic guitar.
ex.ko: 그는 어쿠스틱 기타 연주를 선호한다.

acoustically.en: in a way that relates to sound or hearing

ex.en: The room is acoustically designed for concerts.
ex.ko: 그 방은 콘서트에 맞게 음향학적으로 설계되었다.

ex.en: She studied acoustics at university.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대학에서 음향학을 공부했다.

acoustics.en: the qualities of a room or building that affect how sound is heard in it

ex.en: The acoustics in this concert hall are amazing.
ex.ko: 이 콘서트홀의 음향은 놀랍다.

acre.en: a unit of area used for measuring land, equal to about 4,047 square meters

ex.en: The farm covers over a hundred acres.
ex.ko: 그 농장은 100에이커가 넘는다.

acutely.en: in an extreme or intense way

ex.en: She was acutely aware of everyone staring at her.
ex.ko: 그녀는 모두가 자신을 쳐다보고 있다는 것을 극도로 의식했다.

ex.en: The patient became acutely ill overnight.
ex.ko: 그 환자는 하룻밤 사이 급성으로 병이 악화되었다.

adjoin.en: be next to or touching something

ex.en: Our garden adjoins the park.
ex.ko: 우리 정원은 공원과 맞닿아 있다.

adjoining.en: next to or connected to something

ex.en: They booked adjoining rooms at the hotel.
ex.ko: 그들은 호텔에서 서로 붙어 있는 방을 예약했다.

adjustable.en: able to be changed or adjusted to fit different needs

ex.en: The office chair has an adjustable height.
ex.ko: 그 사무용 의자는 높이 조절이 가능하다.

adrenaline.en: a hormone released in response to stress or excitement that increases heart rate and energy

ex.en: Her adrenaline was pumping before the race.
ex.ko: 경주 전 그녀의 아드레날린이 솟구쳤다.

advantageous.en: helpful or likely to bring benefits

ex.en: It would be advantageous to learn a second language.
ex.ko: 제2외국어를 배우는 것은 유리할 것이다.

adversary.en: a person who opposes or competes with another

ex.en: The two candidates were fierce political adversaries.
ex.ko: 그 두 후보는 치열한 정치적 라이벌이었다.

advisory.en: giving advice rather than making decisions

ex.en: She serves in an advisory role for the committee.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 위원회에서 자문 역할을 맡고 있다.

advisory.en: an official warning about a possible danger

ex.en: The weather service issued a storm advisory.
ex.ko: 기상청은 폭풍 주의보를 발령했다.

aesthetic.en: relating to beauty or the appreciation of beauty

ex.en: The building was designed with aesthetic appeal in mind.
ex.ko: 그 건물은 미적 매력을 염두에 두고 설계되었다.

aesthetic.en: a particular style or set of ideas about beauty and taste

ex.en: The film has a dark, moody aesthetic.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 어둡고 몽환적인 미학을 지니고 있다.

aesthetically.en: in a way that relates to beauty or artistic taste

ex.en: The renovated house looks aesthetically pleasing.
ex.ko: 리모델링된 그 집은 미적으로 보기 좋다.

ex.en: We spent an agreeable evening chatting by the fire.
ex.ko: 우리는 난롯가에서 이야기를 나누며 유쾌한 저녁을 보냈다.

ex.en: He seemed agreeable to the new proposal.
ex.ko: 그는 새 제안에 동의하는 듯 보였다.

aimless.en: without a clear purpose or direction

ex.en: He spent his twenties in aimless wandering.
ex.ko: 그는 20대를 목적 없이 방황하며 보냈다.

aimlessly.en: without a clear purpose or direction

ex.en: She wandered aimlessly through the streets.
ex.ko: 그녀는 정처 없이 거리를 헤맸다.

alienate.en: cause someone to feel isolated or unwelcome, or to become unfriendly

ex.en: His harsh comments alienated many of his colleagues.
ex.ko: 그의 가혹한 발언은 많은 동료들을 멀어지게 만들었다.

align.en: arrange things so they form a straight line or are in the correct position relative to each other

ex.en: Make sure the shelves are aligned properly.
ex.ko: 선반들이 제대로 정렬되어 있는지 확인해라.

align.en: support or join a particular group, party, or cause

ex.en: The senator aligned herself with the reform movement.
ex.ko: 그 상원의원은 개혁 운동과 뜻을 같이했다.

alignment.en: the correct or exact position of things in relation to each other, especially in a straight line

ex.en: The wheels are out of alignment.
ex.ko: 바퀴 정렬이 맞지 않는다.

alignment.en: agreement or cooperation between people, groups, or countries with a common goal

ex.en: The two parties formed a political alignment.
ex.ko: 그 두 정당은 정치적 제휴를 맺었다.

allege.en: state something as a fact without providing proof

ex.en: The report alleges that the company broke the law.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 그 회사가 법을 위반했다고 주장한다.

alleviate.en: make pain, suffering, or a problem less severe

ex.en: The medicine helped alleviate her back pain.
ex.ko: 그 약은 그녀의 허리 통증을 완화하는 데 도움이 되었다.

altruism.en: unselfish concern for the well-being of others

ex.en: Her volunteer work was a pure act of altruism.
ex.ko: 그녀의 자원봉사 활동은 순수한 이타주의의 발로였다.

altruistic.en: showing unselfish concern for the well-being of others

ex.en: He made an altruistic decision to donate his savings.
ex.ko: 그는 저축한 돈을 기부하는 이타적인 결정을 내렸다.

amateur.en: a person who does something as a hobby, not as a profession

ex.en: He's just an amateur photographer, but his photos are stunning.
ex.ko: 그는 그저 아마추어 사진가일 뿐이지만 사진이 정말 멋지다.

ex.en: The repair job looked pretty amateur.
ex.ko: 그 수리 작업은 꽤 미숙해 보였다.

amid.en: in the middle of or surrounded by

ex.en: The city remained calm amid the political crisis.
ex.ko: 그 도시는 정치적 위기 속에서도 평온을 유지했다.

amplification.en: the process of making a sound or signal stronger

ex.en: The speakers provide clear amplification of the singer's voice.
ex.ko: 그 스피커는 가수의 목소리를 선명하게 증폭해 준다.

amplification.en: the addition of extra detail or explanation to make something clearer

ex.en: The report needs further amplification on the budget figures.
ex.ko: 그 보고서는 예산 수치에 대한 추가적인 상술이 필요하다.

amply.en: in a way that is more than enough

ex.en: The team was amply rewarded for their hard work.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 노력에 대해 충분한 보상을 받았다.

anchored.en: fixed firmly in place, or (of a ship) held in place by an anchor

ex.en: The boat remained anchored in the harbor overnight.
ex.ko: 그 배는 밤새 항구에 정박해 있었다.

animatedly.en: in a lively and enthusiastic way

ex.en: They were talking animatedly about the game.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 경기에 대해 열띠게 이야기하고 있었다.

anthropologist.en: a scientist who studies human societies and cultures

ex.en: The anthropologist spent years living with the tribe.
ex.ko: 그 인류학자는 그 부족과 함께 여러 해를 살았다.

anthropology.en: the scientific study of human societies, cultures, and their development

ex.en: She majored in anthropology in college.
ex.ko: 그녀는 대학에서 인류학을 전공했다.

anticlimactic.en: disappointing because it happens after something more exciting was expected

ex.en: After all the buildup, the ending felt anticlimactic.
ex.ko: 그렇게 기대를 모았는데도 결말은 김빠지게 느껴졌다.

antics.en: silly or funny behavior, especially intended to entertain or annoy

ex.en: The children's antics kept everyone laughing.
ex.ko: 아이들의 우스꽝스러운 행동에 모두가 계속 웃었다.

antiquated.en: old-fashioned and no longer useful or suitable

ex.en: The factory still uses antiquated machinery.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 여전히 구식 기계를 사용한다.

antiquity.en: the ancient past, especially the times of ancient Greece and Rome

ex.en: The ruins date back to antiquity.
ex.ko: 그 유적은 고대까지 거슬러 올라간다.

antiquity.en: the quality of being very old

ex.en: The temple is remarkable for its antiquity.
ex.ko: 그 사원은 유구한 역사로 유명하다.

ex.en: The scale of the destruction appalled everyone.
ex.ko: 그 파괴의 규모는 모두를 경악하게 했다.

ex.en: The living conditions in the camp were appalling.
ex.ko: 그 수용소의 생활 환경은 끔찍했다.

ex.en: The team played appallingly in the final match.
ex.ko: 그 팀은 결승전에서 끔찍할 정도로 형편없이 경기했다.

appliance.en: a device or machine, especially one used in the home, such as a refrigerator or washing machine

ex.en: They bought several new kitchen appliances.
ex.ko: 그들은 새 주방 가전제품을 여러 개 샀다.

apprehensive.en: worried or nervous about something that might happen

ex.en: She felt apprehensive about the job interview.
ex.ko: 그녀는 취업 면접을 앞두고 불안했다.

apprentice.en: a person who works for someone skilled in order to learn a trade

ex.en: He started as an apprentice to a master carpenter.
ex.ko: 그는 목수 장인 밑에서 견습생으로 일을 시작했다.

apprentice.en: work or place someone as an apprentice to learn a trade

ex.en: She was apprenticed to a jeweler at sixteen.
ex.ko: 그녀는 열여섯 살에 보석 세공사 밑에서 견습을 시작했다.

apprenticeship.en: a period of time spent working for someone skilled to learn their trade

ex.en: He completed a five-year apprenticeship as an electrician.
ex.ko: 그는 전기 기술자로서 5년간의 견습 기간을 마쳤다.

apt.en: exactly suitable or fitting for a particular situation

ex.en: She made an apt comparison between the two novels.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 두 소설 사이에 적절한 비교를 했다.

apt.en: having a natural tendency to do something

ex.en: He is apt to forget people's names.
ex.ko: 그는 사람들 이름을 잘 잊어버리는 경향이 있다.

apt to.en: likely to do something, especially something negative

ex.en: Metal roofs are apt to rust in humid climates.
ex.ko: 금속 지붕은 습한 기후에서 녹슬기 쉽다.

ex.en: She painted her bedroom walls aqua.
ex.ko: 그녀는 침실 벽을 청록색으로 칠했다.

aquarium.en: a tank or building where fish and other water animals are kept

ex.en: We took the kids to the aquarium on Saturday.
ex.ko: 우리는 토요일에 아이들을 데리고 수족관에 갔다.

artistically.en: in a way that shows artistic skill or creativity

ex.en: The dishes were artistically arranged on the plate.
ex.ko: 그 요리들은 접시 위에 예술적으로 담겨 있었다.

assertion.en: a strong statement that something is true

ex.en: He made the assertion that the theory was flawed.
ex.ko: 그는 그 이론에 결함이 있다고 단언했다.

astray.en: away from the correct path or direction

ex.en: The map led us astray, and we ended up lost.
ex.ko: 그 지도 때문에 우리는 길을 잘못 들어 결국 길을 잃었다.

ex.en: His friends led him astray during his teenage years.
ex.ko: 그의 친구들은 그가 십대일 때 그를 나쁜 길로 이끌었다.

atmospheric.en: relating to the atmosphere, the layer of gases surrounding the Earth

ex.en: Atmospheric pressure drops as you climb higher.
ex.ko: 고도가 높아질수록 대기압이 낮아진다.

atmospheric.en: creating a pleasant or interesting mood or atmosphere

ex.en: The film has a wonderfully atmospheric soundtrack.
ex.ko: 그 영화는 멋지게 분위기 있는 사운드트랙을 가지고 있다.

avid.en: having or showing great enthusiasm for something

ex.en: She is an avid reader who finishes a book every week.
ex.ko: 그녀는 매주 책 한 권을 끝내는 열렬한 독서가이다.

baldly.en: in a very direct and plain way, without softening the words

ex.en: He baldly stated that the project had failed.
ex.ko: 그는 그 프로젝트가 실패했다고 노골적으로 말했다.

batter.en: hit someone or something hard and repeatedly

ex.en: Waves battered the small boat all night.
ex.ko: 파도가 밤새 그 작은 배를 강타했다.

batter.en: a liquid mixture of flour, eggs, and milk used in cooking, e.g., for pancakes

ex.en: Dip the chicken in batter before frying.
ex.ko: 튀기기 전에 닭고기를 반죽에 담가라.

batter.en: (in baseball) the player who is hitting the ball

ex.en: The batter hit a home run in the ninth inning.
ex.ko: 그 타자는 9회에 홈런을 쳤다.

battered.en: damaged from being used roughly or hit repeatedly

ex.en: He drove an old, battered pickup truck.
ex.ko: 그는 심하게 낡은 픽업트럭을 몰았다.

ex.en: The shelter provides support for battered women.
ex.ko: 그 보호소는 학대받은 여성들을 지원한다.

ex.en: The bearded man greeted us at the door.
ex.ko: 수염 난 남자가 문에서 우리를 맞았다.

beforehand.en: before a particular time or event

ex.en: Please let us know beforehand if you can't attend.
ex.ko: 참석할 수 없다면 미리 알려주세요.

beguiling.en: very attractive or charming in a way that arouses interest

ex.en: There is something beguiling about her smile.
ex.ko: 그녀의 미소에는 매혹적인 무언가가 있다.

beguiling.en: attractive in a misleading way that can deceive someone

ex.en: The salesman gave a beguiling but ultimately false promise.
ex.ko: 그 영업사원은 매혹적이지만 결국 거짓인 약속을 했다.

ex.en: She smiled beguilingly at the crowd.
ex.ko: 그녀는 청중을 향해 매혹적으로 미소지었다.

believably.en: in a way that seems true or convincing

ex.en: The actor portrayed the villain believably.
ex.ko: 그 배우는 악당 역을 그럴듯하게 연기했다.

benign.en: (of a disease or tumor) not dangerous to health; not cancerous

ex.en: Fortunately, the tumor was benign.
ex.ko: 다행히 그 종양은 양성이었다.

ex.en: The old professor had a benign smile.
ex.ko: 그 나이 든 교수는 온화한 미소를 지었다.

benignly.en: in a kind and gentle way

ex.en: She looked at the child benignly.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 아이를 자애롭게 바라보았다.

bequeath.en: leave property or money to someone in a will after your death

ex.en: He bequeathed his entire fortune to charity.
ex.ko: 그는 자신의 전 재산을 자선단체에 유증했다.

billiards.en: a game played on a table where players use cue sticks to hit balls

ex.en: They played billiards at the club after dinner.
ex.ko: 그들은 저녁 식사 후 클럽에서 당구를 쳤다.

blankness.en: the state of showing no expression, emotion, or thought

ex.en: There was a blankness in her eyes after the shock.
ex.ko: 충격을 받은 후 그녀의 눈에는 공허함이 서려 있었다.

ex.en: Lying on the beach felt like pure bliss.
ex.ko: 해변에 누워 있으니 그야말로 더없는 행복이었다.

blister.en: a small, painful swelling on the skin filled with fluid, caused by rubbing or burning

ex.en: The new shoes gave her a blister on her heel.
ex.ko: 새 신발 때문에 그녀는 발뒤꿈치에 물집이 생겼다.

blister.en: develop a blister, or cause one to develop

ex.en: Her hands blistered after hours of rowing.
ex.ko: 몇 시간 동안 노를 저은 후 그녀의 손에 물집이 생겼다.

blithely.en: in a carefree way, without worrying about consequences

ex.en: He blithely ignored all the warnings and went ahead.
ex.ko: 그는 모든 경고를 태평하게 무시하고 강행했다.

blockbuster.en: a movie, book, or other product that is extremely successful and popular

ex.en: The summer blockbuster broke box office records.
ex.ko: 그 여름 블록버스터는 흥행 기록을 깼다.

bouquet.en: a bunch of flowers arranged attractively, often given as a gift

ex.en: He gave her a bouquet of roses.
ex.ko: 그는 그녀에게 장미 꽃다발을 주었다.

bouquet.en: the characteristic smell of a wine

ex.en: The wine has a rich, fruity bouquet.
ex.ko: 그 와인은 풍부하고 과일 향이 나는 향을 지녔다.

brainwash.en: force someone to accept certain beliefs through repeated pressure, often through coercion

ex.en: The cult brainwashed its members into obedience.
ex.ko: 그 사이비 종교는 신도들을 세뇌시켜 복종하게 만들었다.

brainwashing.en: the process of forcing someone to accept certain beliefs through repeated pressure

ex.en: The documentary exposed the cult's brainwashing techniques.
ex.ko: 그 다큐멘터리는 그 사이비 종교의 세뇌 기법을 폭로했다.

bribe.en: money or a gift given to someone to persuade them to do something dishonest

ex.en: The official was caught accepting a bribe.
ex.ko: 그 공무원은 뇌물을 받다가 적발되었다.

bribe.en: give someone money or a gift to persuade them to do something dishonest

ex.en: They tried to bribe the security guard.
ex.ko: 그들은 경비원을 매수하려 했다.

bribery.en: the practice of giving or receiving bribes

ex.en: The politician resigned after a bribery scandal.
ex.ko: 그 정치인은 뇌물수수 스캔들 이후 사임했다.

bubbly.en: full of small bubbles, as in a carbonated drink

ex.en: She poured a glass of bubbly lemonade.
ex.ko: 그녀는 거품이 이는 레모네이드를 한 잔 따랐다.

bubbly.en: having a lively and cheerful personality

ex.en: The new intern has a bubbly personality.
ex.ko: 그 신입 인턴은 발랄한 성격을 가지고 있다.

buffer.en: something that reduces the shock or impact between two things or protects against harm

ex.en: Savings can act as a buffer against financial emergencies.
ex.ko: 저축은 재정적 위기에 대한 완충 장치 역할을 할 수 있다.

buffer.en: a temporary storage area in a computer's memory for data being transferred

ex.en: The video kept stopping to buffer.
ex.ko: 그 동영상은 계속 버퍼링으로 멈췄다.

buffer.en: protect someone or something from difficulty or harm

ex.en: The new policy is designed to buffer small businesses from rising costs.
ex.ko: 그 새 정책은 소상공인들을 비용 상승으로부터 보호하기 위해 마련되었다.

buffoon.en: a person who behaves in a silly or ridiculous way, often for laughs

ex.en: He acted like a buffoon at the party.
ex.ko: 그는 파티에서 바보처럼 행동했다.

bulk.en: the majority or largest part of something

ex.en: The bulk of the work is already done.
ex.ko: 작업의 대부분은 이미 끝났다.

bulk.en: the large size or mass of something

ex.en: The furniture's bulk made it hard to move.
ex.ko: 그 가구는 부피가 커서 옮기기 힘들었다.

bulk up.en: increase in size or muscle mass, especially through exercise or eating

ex.en: He's been bulking up at the gym for months.
ex.ko: 그는 몇 달째 헬스장에서 근육을 키우고 있다.

bulky.en: large and heavy in a way that is difficult to carry or store

ex.en: The old television was too bulky to move alone.
ex.ko: 그 낡은 텔레비전은 부피가 너무 커서 혼자 옮길 수 없었다.

bureaucratic.en: relating to complicated official rules and procedures, especially in government

ex.en: Getting the permit involved a lot of bureaucratic red tape.
ex.ko: 허가를 받는 데는 많은 관료적 절차가 뒤따랐다.

bureaucratically.en: in a way that involves complicated official rules and procedures

ex.en: The application was handled bureaucratically and slowly.
ex.ko: 그 신청은 관료적으로 느리게 처리되었다.

buzzer.en: a device that makes a buzzing sound as a signal

ex.en: Press the buzzer to be let in.
ex.ko: 들어가려면 버저를 누르세요.

bypass.en: avoid something by going around it or skipping it

ex.en: The new software bypasses the usual login process.
ex.ko: 그 새 소프트웨어는 일반적인 로그인 절차를 건너뛴다.

bypass.en: a road that goes around a town so traffic does not need to pass through it

ex.en: Take the bypass to avoid the city center.
ex.ko: 시내를 피하려면 우회 도로를 타세요.

calorie.en: a unit used to measure the amount of energy in food

ex.en: This snack has only 100 calories.
ex.ko: 이 간식은 칼로리가 100칼로리밖에 안 된다.

candid.en: honest and direct, without hiding anything

ex.en: She gave a candid account of her struggles.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 어려움에 대해 솔직하게 이야기했다.

candidly.en: in an honest and direct way

ex.en: Candidly, I don't think the plan will work.
ex.ko: 솔직히 말하면, 나는 그 계획이 성공할 것 같지 않다.

canoe.cue: 노를 저어 타는 좁고 가벼운 배
canoe.en: a light, narrow boat moved using a paddle

ex.en: They paddled the canoe down the river.
ex.ko: 그들은 카누를 저어 강을 따라 내려갔다.

carpenter.en: a person who makes or repairs wooden objects and structures

ex.en: The carpenter built a new bookshelf for us.
ex.ko: 그 목수는 우리를 위해 새 책장을 만들어 주었다.

carpentry.en: the skill or work of making and repairing wooden objects

ex.en: He learned carpentry from his grandfather.
ex.ko: 그는 할아버지에게서 목공을 배웠다.

carrier.en: a person or company that transports goods, people, or messages

ex.en: The package was delivered by a private carrier.
ex.ko: 그 소포는 민간 운송업체를 통해 배달되었다.

carrier.cue: 병을 옮기지만 본인은 증상 없는 사람
carrier.en: a person or animal that carries a disease without showing symptoms but can pass it to others

ex.en: He was found to be a carrier of the virus.
ex.ko: 그는 그 바이러스의 보균자로 밝혀졌다.

cavity.en: a hole in a tooth caused by decay

ex.en: The dentist found two cavities during the checkup.
ex.ko: 치과의사는 검진 중 충치 두 개를 발견했다.

cavity.en: a hollow or empty space inside something

ex.en: The nasal cavity connects to the throat.
ex.ko: 비강은 목구멍과 연결되어 있다.

ex.en: The ceaseless noise from the construction site was exhausting.
ex.ko: 공사장에서 나는 끊임없는 소음은 지치게 만들었다.

ex.en: She worked ceaselessly to finish the project on time.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 프로젝트를 제때 끝내기 위해 끊임없이 일했다.

cessation.en: the stopping of an action or process

ex.en: The two sides agreed to a cessation of hostilities.
ex.ko: 양측은 적대 행위의 중단에 합의했다.

chaotically.en: in a way that is completely disorganized or confused

ex.en: People ran chaotically out of the building during the fire alarm.
ex.ko: 화재 경보가 울리자 사람들은 혼란스럽게 건물 밖으로 뛰쳐나갔다.

characterization.en: the way a writer or actor creates and presents a character

ex.en: The novel is praised for its rich characterization.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 풍부한 인물 묘사로 호평받는다.

characterization.en: a description of someone or something in a particular way

ex.en: He objected to the media's characterization of the event as a riot.
ex.ko: 그는 그 사건을 폭동으로 규정한 언론의 서술에 반발했다.

charter.en: rent an entire vehicle such as a plane, bus, or boat for private use

ex.en: We chartered a boat for the day.
ex.ko: 우리는 하루 동안 배를 전세 냈다.

charter.en: an official document that states the rights and purpose of an organization

ex.en: The company's charter outlines its mission.
ex.ko: 그 회사의 정관은 회사의 사명을 명시한다.

chauffer.cue: 부유한 사람을 태우고 운전해 주는 사람
chauffer.en: a person employed to drive someone else's car

ex.en: A chauffeur picked her up from the airport.
ex.ko: 운전기사가 공항으로 그녀를 마중 나왔다.

chauffer.en: drive someone somewhere as a service

ex.en: He chauffeured his boss around the city all day.
ex.ko: 그는 하루 종일 상사를 태우고 시내를 돌았다.

ex.en: There was a chill in the air this morning.
ex.ko: 오늘 아침 공기에 냉기가 감돌았다.

chill.en: cause someone to feel fear, a thrill of fright, or discouragement

ex.en: His cold stare chilled her to the bone.
ex.ko: 그의 차가운 눈빛은 그녀를 소름 끼치게 했다.

chill.en: make something cold, especially food or drinks

ex.en: Chill the wine before serving.
ex.ko: 제공하기 전에 와인을 차게 식혀라.

ex.en: It's a bit chilly outside today.
ex.ko: 오늘 밖은 좀 쌀쌀하다.

chilly.en: unfriendly or lacking warmth in manner

ex.en: She gave him a chilly reception.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그를 냉랭하게 맞이했다.

chisel.en: carve or shape wood or stone using a chisel

ex.en: The sculptor chiseled the marble into a statue.
ex.ko: 그 조각가는 대리석을 끌로 깎아 조각상을 만들었다.

chisel.en: cheat someone out of money or property (informal)

ex.en: He was accused of chiseling investors out of their savings.
ex.ko: 그는 투자자들의 저축금을 사취했다는 혐의를 받았다.

chiselled.en: (of facial features) strong and clearly defined, as if carved

ex.en: He had chiselled cheekbones and a strong jaw.
ex.ko: 그는 조각한 듯한 광대뼈와 강인한 턱선을 가지고 있었다.

churn.en: move or cause to move around violently

ex.en: The rough sea churned beneath the boat.
ex.ko: 거친 바다가 배 아래에서 세차게 뒤흔들렸다.

circulate.en: spread from person to person or place to place

ex.en: Rumors began to circulate about the merger.
ex.ko: 합병에 관한 소문이 돌기 시작했다.

circulate.en: move continuously through a closed system, returning to the starting point

ex.en: Blood circulates through the body.
ex.ko: 혈액은 몸 전체를 순환한다.

circulation.en: the movement of blood through the body, or of something moving through a system

ex.en: Exercise improves blood circulation.
ex.ko: 운동은 혈액 순환을 개선한다.

circulation.en: the number of copies of a newspaper or magazine that are sold

ex.en: The magazine's circulation has dropped in recent years.
ex.ko: 그 잡지의 발행 부수는 최근 몇 년 사이 감소했다.

citation.en: a reference to a source of information used in a piece of writing

ex.en: The essay includes citations from several academic journals.
ex.ko: 그 에세이는 여러 학술지의 인용을 포함하고 있다.

citation.cue: 용감한 행동 등에 대한 공식 표창
citation.en: an official award or public statement recognizing someone's brave or exceptional action

ex.en: The firefighter received a citation for bravery.
ex.ko: 그 소방관은 용감한 행동으로 표창을 받았다.

citation.en: an official notice ordering someone to appear in court or pay a fine for breaking a law

ex.en: She got a citation for parking in a no-parking zone.
ex.ko: 그녀는 주차 금지 구역에 주차해서 위반 딱지를 받았다.

cite.en: quote or refer to something as evidence or example

ex.en: She cited several studies to support her argument.
ex.ko: 그녀는 자신의 주장을 뒷받침하기 위해 여러 연구를 인용했다.

cite.en: officially order someone to appear in court

ex.en: He was cited for violating city ordinances.
ex.ko: 그는 시 조례를 위반해 소환되었다.

clammy.en: unpleasantly damp and cold, usually referring to skin

ex.en: His palms were clammy with nervousness.
ex.ko: 그는 긴장해서 손바닥이 축축하고 차가웠다.

clan.en: a group of families that are closely related, especially in Scottish or tribal tradition

ex.en: The MacGregor clan gathered for the annual reunion.
ex.ko: 맥그리거 씨족은 연례 모임을 위해 모였다.

classy.en: stylish, elegant, and of high quality

ex.en: She wore a classy black dress to the gala.
ex.ko: 그녀는 갈라 행사에 세련된 검은 드레스를 입고 갔다.

claustrophobia.en: an extreme fear of being in enclosed or crowded spaces

ex.en: His claustrophobia made the elevator ride unbearable.
ex.ko: 그의 폐소공포증 때문에 엘리베이터를 타는 것이 견딜 수 없었다.

claustrophobic.en: suffering from an extreme fear of enclosed spaces

ex.en: She's claustrophobic and avoids small elevators.
ex.ko: 그녀는 폐소공포증이 있어서 좁은 엘리베이터를 피한다.

claustrophobic.en: (of a place) uncomfortably enclosed or crowded, causing feelings of being trapped

ex.en: The tiny basement room felt claustrophobic.
ex.ko: 그 작은 지하 방은 갑갑하게 느껴졌다.

cleanly.en: smoothly and without difficulty or mess

ex.en: The knife sliced cleanly through the fabric.
ex.ko: 그 칼은 천을 깔끔하게 잘랐다.

clench.en: close or hold something tightly, such as your fist or teeth

ex.en: He clenched his fists in anger.
ex.ko: 그는 화가 나서 주먹을 꽉 쥐었다.

cleverness.en: the quality of being intelligent, quick-witted, or skillful

ex.en: Her cleverness helped her solve the puzzle quickly.
ex.ko: 그녀의 영리함 덕분에 퍼즐을 빨리 풀 수 있었다.

climactic.en: relating to or forming the most exciting or important point of something

ex.en: The film's climactic battle scene lasted twenty minutes.
ex.ko: 그 영화의 절정 전투 장면은 20분 동안 이어졌다.

climax.en: the most exciting, important, or intense point of something, such as a story or event

ex.en: The novel builds slowly to its climax.
ex.ko: 그 소설은 절정을 향해 서서히 고조된다.

climax.en: reach the most exciting, important, or decisive point

ex.en: The tournament climaxed with a dramatic final match.
ex.ko: 그 대회는 극적인 결승전으로 절정에 달했다.

clinical.en: relating to the direct observation and treatment of patients, as in a hospital or clinic

ex.en: The drug passed several clinical trials.
ex.ko: 그 약은 여러 임상 시험을 통과했다.

clinical.en: coldly detached and analytical, without emotion

ex.en: He gave a clinical assessment of the situation.
ex.ko: 그는 그 상황에 대해 냉정하고 객관적인 평가를 내렸다.

cloak.cue: 몸에 걸치는 소매 없는 긴 겉옷
cloak.en: a loose outer garment without sleeves, worn over the shoulders

ex.en: She wrapped herself in a long black cloak.
ex.ko: 그녀는 긴 검은 망토로 몸을 감쌌다.

cloak.en: hide or disguise something, often figuratively

ex.en: The scandal was cloaked in secrecy for years.
ex.ko: 그 스캔들은 오랫동안 비밀에 가려져 있었다.

closeness.en: a feeling of being emotionally close to someone

ex.en: Their closeness grew after years of friendship.
ex.ko: 그들의 친밀함은 오랜 우정 끝에 깊어졌다.

closeness.en: the state of being physically near to something

ex.en: The closeness of the hotel to the beach was convenient.
ex.ko: 호텔이 해변과 가까운 것이 편리했다.

clutch.en: hold onto something tightly, often out of fear or desperation

ex.en: She clutched her bag tightly as she walked through the crowd.
ex.ko: 그녀는 군중 사이를 걸으며 가방을 꽉 움켜쥐었다.

clutch.en: the pedal or mechanism in a vehicle that connects and disconnects the engine from the wheels

ex.en: He pressed the clutch before shifting gears.
ex.ko: 그는 기어를 바꾸기 전에 클러치를 밟았다.

clutch.en: a tense or critical situation, especially in sports

ex.en: She always performs well in the clutch.
ex.ko: 그녀는 결정적인 순간에 항상 실력을 발휘한다.

clutter.en: an untidy collection of things lying around

ex.en: There was so much clutter on her desk that she couldn't find anything.
ex.ko: 그녀의 책상에는 잡동사니가 너무 많아서 아무것도 찾을 수 없었다.

clutter.en: fill a space in a messy, disorganized way

ex.en: Old papers cluttered every surface in the office.
ex.ko: 낡은 서류들이 사무실의 모든 표면을 어지럽혔다.

collaborate.en: work together with others on a shared project or goal

ex.en: The two universities collaborated on the research project.
ex.ko: 그 두 대학은 그 연구 프로젝트에서 협력했다.

collaboration.en: the act of working together with others on a shared project

ex.en: The album was a collaboration between two famous singers.
ex.ko: 그 앨범은 두 유명 가수 간의 공동 작업이었다.

ex.en: They took a collaborative approach to solving the problem.
ex.ko: 그들은 그 문제를 해결하는 데 협력적인 접근 방식을 취했다.

collaborator.en: a person who works together with someone else on a project

ex.en: She found a great collaborator for her new film.
ex.ko: 그녀는 새 영화를 위한 훌륭한 협력자를 찾았다.

commend.en: praise someone or something formally, or recommend them

ex.en: The teacher commended the students for their hard work.
ex.ko: 선생님은 학생들의 노력을 칭찬했다.

commendation.en: official praise or an award given for good work or bravery

ex.en: He received a commendation for his outstanding service.
ex.ko: 그는 뛰어난 봉사에 대해 표창을 받았다.

commercially.en: in a way that relates to buying and selling for profit

ex.en: The invention was never commercially successful.
ex.ko: 그 발명품은 상업적으로 성공하지 못했다.

ex.en: The law compels drivers to wear seat belts.
ex.ko: 그 법은 운전자들이 안전벨트를 매도록 강제한다.

complementary.en: combining well with something else to complete or improve it

ex.en: The two colors are complementary and look great together.
ex.ko: 그 두 색은 서로 보완적이어서 함께 있으면 멋져 보인다.

compliance.en: the act of obeying a rule, law, or request

ex.en: The factory was inspected for compliance with safety regulations.
ex.ko: 그 공장은 안전 규정 준수 여부를 점검받았다.

compliant.en: willing to obey rules or requests

ex.en: The new product is fully compliant with international standards.
ex.ko: 그 신제품은 국제 표준을 완전히 준수한다.

compliantly.en: in a way that obeys rules or requests without resistance

ex.en: He compliantly followed every instruction given.
ex.ko: 그는 주어진 모든 지시를 순순히 따랐다.

ex.en: She made several complimentary remarks about the presentation.
ex.ko: 그녀는 그 발표에 대해 여러 칭찬의 말을 했다.

ex.en: The hotel offers a complimentary breakfast.
ex.ko: 그 호텔은 무료 조식을 제공한다.

comply.en: act according to someone's rules, wishes, or commands

ex.en: All employees must comply with the new safety policy.
ex.ko: 모든 직원은 새 안전 정책을 준수해야 한다.

comprise.en: consist of or be made up of particular parts

ex.en: The committee comprises ten members.
ex.ko: 그 위원회는 10명의 위원으로 구성된다.

compromised.en: made weaker or less effective, often because of danger or damage

ex.en: The building's structure was seriously compromised by the earthquake.
ex.ko: 그 건물의 구조는 지진으로 심각하게 손상되었다.

compulsion.en: a strong urge to do something, especially something irrational

ex.en: He felt a sudden compulsion to check his phone.
ex.ko: 그는 갑자기 휴대폰을 확인하고 싶은 충동을 느꼈다.

compulsion.en: the use of force to make someone do something

ex.en: There is no compulsion to attend the meeting.
ex.ko: 그 회의에 참석해야 하는 강제성은 없다.

con.en: trick someone, especially to get money from them

ex.en: He conned elderly people out of their savings.
ex.ko: 그는 노인들을 속여 저축금을 빼앗았다.

con.en: a dishonest scheme to trick someone, especially for money

ex.en: The investment turned out to be a con.
ex.ko: 그 투자는 결국 사기로 밝혀졌다.

con.en: an argument or reason against something (used especially in 'pros and cons')

ex.en: We weighed the pros and cons before deciding.
ex.ko: 우리는 결정을 내리기 전에 찬반 양론을 따져 보았다.

concerto.en: a piece of music written for one or more solo instruments and an orchestra

ex.en: She performed a violin concerto with the symphony.
ex.ko: 그녀는 교향악단과 함께 바이올린 협주곡을 연주했다.

concur.en: have the same opinion as someone else; agree

ex.en: The judges concurred on the final decision.
ex.ko: 심사위원들은 최종 결정에 대해 의견이 일치했다.

conditional.en: true or possible only if something else happens first

ex.en: The job offer is conditional on passing a background check.
ex.ko: 그 채용 제안은 신원 조회 통과를 조건으로 한다.

ex.en: The loan was approved conditionally.
ex.ko: 그 대출은 조건부로 승인되었다.

confide.en: tell someone a secret or personal matter, trusting them not to share it

ex.en: She confided in her best friend about her worries.
ex.ko: 그녀는 가장 친한 친구에게 자신의 걱정을 털어놓았다.

confide.en: entrust someone with a responsibility or task

ex.en: The document was confided to his care.
ex.ko: 그 문서는 그의 관리에 맡겨졌다.
